intro: Heptapleurum archboldianum, also known as the Waigeu fig tree, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum archboldianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
